pathway: P4a (sync execute_code; FM-05 pathway family is P4a/P4b)

fidelity.repl_code_accuracy: PASS: The fixture encodes the intended partial-state FM-05 variant: multi-statement sync code assigns `x` and `y` before failing on `undefined_var` (tests_rlm_adk/fixtures/provider_fake/repl_runtime_error_partial_state.json:27). PASS: Because no `llm_query` calls exist, REPLTool routes through sync `execute_code` (`has_llm_calls(code)` false path) (rlm_adk/tools/repl_tool.py:108-119; rlm_adk/repl/ast_rewriter.py:15-36). PASS: Sync locals are only merged into `self.locals` after successful `exec`; exception path skips that merge (rlm_adk/repl/local_repl.py:294-309).
fidelity.response_mock_accuracy: PASS: Response topology is coherent for a two-iteration self-correction flow: reasoning tool call -> corrected tool call -> final text with `FINAL(Result: 90)` (tests_rlm_adk/fixtures/provider_fake/repl_runtime_error_partial_state.json:11-112). PASS: Expected contract values (`total_iterations=2`, `total_model_calls=3`) are consistent with this topology and are exercised by tests (tests_rlm_adk/fixtures/provider_fake/repl_runtime_error_partial_state.json:108-112; tests_rlm_adk/test_fmea_e2e.py:1596-1607).
fidelity.fault_injection_accuracy: PASS: FM-05 here is code-intrinsic runtime failure, so no synthetic HTTP/transport fault injection is needed (`fault_injections` is intentionally empty) (tests_rlm_adk/fixtures/provider_fake/repl_runtime_error_partial_state.json:107). PASS: This matches FM-05 definition where runtime exceptions are raised by user code and surfaced through local_repl exception handling (rlm_adk_docs/FMEA/rlm_adk_FMEA.md:116-121; rlm_adk/repl/local_repl.py:305-309).
gaps: FAIL: The class does not explicitly assert `llm_calls_made == False` for the tool responses, so sync-path routing is inferred rather than directly asserted (tests_rlm_adk/test_fmea_e2e.py:1608-1648; rlm_adk/tools/repl_tool.py:93-119,204).

FAIL: The first-tool variables check is key-level (`x` and `y` absent) rather than strict equality (`variables == {}`), which leaves room for unnoticed leakage of other keys (tests_rlm_adk/test_fmea_e2e.py:1633-1641).
FAIL: Team C demo documents FM-05 sync behavior conceptually but does not yet mention this new fixture by name, so traceability is indirect (demo_showboat/demo_fmea_team_c.md:329-341,454-455).
